channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Greetings from Paris, where an art installation transformed the Pont Neuf into a rocky cave
item.description: The oldest bridge in Paris was draped in fabric last month to appear like a mountain cave, in an installation by the artist JR. Unlike a real cave, though, it didn't provide respite from a brutal heat wave.
item.content:encoded: The oldest bridge in Paris was draped in fabric last month to appear like a mountain cave, in an installation by the artist JR. Unlike a real cave, though, it didn't provide respite from a brutal heat wave.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Rosman

item.title: Acting AG Todd Blanche faces a key test. And, ICE pauses most traffic stops
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is set to appear before the Senate for his confirmation hearing. And, Immigration and Customs Enforcement has paused most traffic stops after recent deadly shootings.
item.content:encoded:
Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is set to appear before the Senate for his confirmation hearing. And, Immigration and Customs Enforcement has paused most traffic stops after recent deadly shootings.

(Image credit: Ken Cedeno)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: How to keep cool in this year's extreme summer heat even without air conditioning
item.description: "Extreme heat" is in the forecast this summer. How do people cope if they don't have air conditioning? Here are suggestions from a heat researcher who grew up in a very hot, AC-less place.
item.content:encoded: "Extreme heat" is in the forecast this summer. How do people cope if they don't have air conditioning? Here are suggestions from a heat researcher who grew up in a very hot, AC-less place.
item.dc:creator: Gulrez Shah Azhar

item.title: The U.S. and Iran standoff over the Strait of Hormuz intensifies
item.description: Iran threatened to block all oil exporting routes in the region on Wednesday in response to the U.S. maritime blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
Iran threatened to block all oil exporting routes in the region on Wednesday in response to the U.S. maritime blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: American AI is expensive. Some startups are turning to cheap Chinese models
item.description: AI is a fast-growing business expense. Some companies are cutting costs by switching to cheaper Chinese AI models.
item.content:encoded:
AI is a fast-growing business expense. Some companies are cutting costs by switching to cheaper Chinese AI models.

(Image credit: Imen Ben Youssef/Hans Lucas)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: CDC director nominee Erica Schwartz faces Senate for confirmation hearing
item.description: Trump's nominee faces the Senate health committee for her confirmation hearing on July 15. If confirmed, she will lead an agency dealing with workforce and leadership shortages and new layers of political review.
item.content:encoded:
Trump's nominee faces the Senate health committee for her confirmation hearing on July 15. If confirmed, she will lead an agency dealing with workforce and leadership shortages and new layers of political review.

(Image credit: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Democrats' witness opposing Blanche's confirmation is more than an Epstein survivor
item.description: Dani Bensky and a group of women who were abused by Jeffrey Epstein have formed a bond they call a "survivor sisterhood." They live in two worlds, of advocacy and everyday life and motherhood.
item.content:encoded:
Dani Bensky and a group of women who were abused by Jeffrey Epstein have formed a bond they call a "survivor sisterhood." They live in two worlds, of advocacy and everyday life and motherhood.

(Image credit: Corrie Aune for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Todd Blanche faces high-stakes confirmation hearing for attorney general
item.description: Blanche won Senate confirmation early in President Trump's second term to serve as the No. 2 official at the Justice Department. Now he's facing a contentious hearing as he seeks the top job.
item.content:encoded:
Blanche won Senate confirmation early in President Trump's second term to serve as the No. 2 official at the Justice Department. Now he's facing a contentious hearing as he seeks the top job.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to appear for confirmation hearing, U.S. restarts blockade against Iran amid standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, ICE suspends traffic stops after deadly shootings.
item.content:encoded: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to appear for confirmation hearing, U.S. restarts blockade against Iran amid standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, ICE suspends traffic stops after deadly shootings.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Mystery bidder buys T. rex nicknamed 'Gus' for a record $50 million
item.description: A Tyrannosaurus rex fossil billed as one of the world's largest and most complete specimens was sold for a record $50.1 million Tuesday to a mystery bidder.
item.content:encoded:
A Tyrannosaurus rex fossil billed as one of the world's largest and most complete specimens was sold for a record $50.1 million Tuesday to a mystery bidder.

(Image credit: Matthew Sherman/Sotheby's via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
